commit.message:
ci: add unused NuGet dependency detection with snitch (#1014)

## Summary

Adds [snitch](https://github.com/spectreconsole/snitch) as a dotnet
local tool to detect unused NuGet package dependencies in the solution.

## Changes

- **`.config/dotnet-tools.json`**: Added `snitch` v2.0.0 as a local
dotnet tool
- **`.github/workflows/main.yml`**: Added a CI step in the build job
that runs `dotnet snitch --strict` after build, failing the pipeline if
unused transitive dependencies are found

## Why

This addresses the Agent Readiness signal for "Unused Dependencies
Detection" by adding tooling that actively detects unused NuGet package
references. The `--strict` flag ensures the CI build fails when unused
dependencies are detected, keeping the dependency tree clean.

## Verification

- Tested locally: `dotnet snitch --strict` runs successfully and reports
`Everything looks good!`
- The tool analyzes all projects in the solution and checks for
transitive package dependencies that can be removed

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
* Added CI analysis to detect and report unused NuGet packages during
builds.
* Registered a new local tool (snitch v2.0.0) to support the dependency
analysis and added it to CI operations.
* Improved local tool restore with retry logic and exponential backoff
to reduce transient failures.
* Expanded pipeline path filtering so relevant configuration changes
trigger the full validation pipeline.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

commit.message:
Fix sudo bash freeze deadlock, escaped cgroup tracking, and RPM upgrade hang

Three related fixes for cgroup-based session freeze:

1. Move sudo out of frozen cgroup: after the first child process (bash, etc.)
   is detected, sudo's PID is moved to the parent cgroup so cgroup.freeze only
   affects children.  Without this, sudo was frozen and could never send
   SESSION_END, causing the shipper goroutine to block forever.

2. Track processes that escape the session cgroup (e.g. gvim moved by GNOME/
   systemd to app-*.scope within milliseconds of launch).  The background
   tracker uses /proc/PID/task/PID/children at 10ms intervals, detects escaped
   PIDs via /proc/PID/cgroup, and freezes them via SIGSTOP/SIGCONT instead of
   cgroup.freeze.  Alive check before classifying as escaped prevents short-
   lived subprocesses (rpm, ls, …) from being falsely flagged.

3. Unfreeze all session cgroups on SIGTERM/SIGINT: a global registry of active
   cgroups and a signal handler call cleanupAllCgs() before exit, so that
   processes in frozen cgroups (e.g. rpm scriptlets running "sudo rpm -Uvh …"
   when systemd restarts the shipper) are not left stuck.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove single-version matrix so CI job is named 'test' not 'test (3.12)'

The branch protection required check was named 'test' but the matrix
strategy caused GitHub Actions to emit the check as 'test (3.12)',
meaning the required check never matched and the PR was permanently
blocked. Since we only test against Python 3.12 there is no need for
a matrix — removing it fixes the name and unblocks merge without --admin.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Strengthen instructions to prevent LLM from summarizing tool results

Added explicit instructions in both system prompt and tool result messages:
- DO NOT SUMMARIZE - show complete data
- For 60 jobs, show ALL 60 jobs, not "15 running, 45 pending"
- Copy entire JSON output even if 50+ lines
- Never truncate or condense long lists

This addresses issue where mistral-nemo was "helpfully" summarizing
large result sets instead of presenting complete data to users who
need to see all entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Add suppressHydrationWarning to html/body to prevent DOM duplication

Browser extensions and theme toggles can modify <html>/<body> attributes
before React hydration, causing Error #418 (hydration mismatch). This
triggers React to re-render the entire Suspense boundary, creating
duplicate DOM nodes that appear below the footer.

Adding suppressHydrationWarning is the standard Next.js fix for this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add shared frontend components (recording control, video, nav, widgets, theme)

Unifies near-identical frontend code from femme-voice-coach and
spanish-voice-coach into a single source of truth in voice-core.

Components:
- recording_control.js: unified mic/camera/3-WebSocket control with
  AudioWorklet primary + ScriptProcessor fallback
- audio_worklet.js: PCM processor (Int16 conversion)
- video_features.js: MediaPipe face/pose extraction + calibration
- biofeedback_widgets.js: PitchStrip, ZoneGauge, GestureBars with
  dependency-injected zone classifiers (no hardcoded thresholds)
- nav.js: configurable header with user picker
- user_api.js: fetch wrapper with X-User-Id, WebSocket URL builder
- theme.css: base dark theme with vc-prefixed layout convention

Apps consume via:
  from voice_core import FRONTEND_DIR
  app.mount("/static/core", StaticFiles(directory=FRONTEND_DIR))

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(shell): rename cat alias to bcat to avoid breaking heredocs

Aliasing cat to bat causes zsh to expand it inside $(cat <<EOF)

substitutions, breaking git commits and other tools that rely on cat.

Using a distinct alias name avoids shadowing the POSIX utility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace taker ratio proxy with real WS liquidation aggregation

_fetch_liquidations() now queries liquidation_events table (real WS
forced liquidations) instead of Binance takerlongshortRatio API.
Taker ratio had buyVol ≈ sellVol (ratio ~1.0), making liq_long,
liq_short, and liq_delta charts all identical and useless.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(socket): use polling-first transport for relay compatibility

Socket.IO v4 with transports:['websocket','polling'] does NOT fall back
to polling on connect_error — it keeps retrying WebSocket. nexus-relay
strips the Upgrade header (hop-by-hop), so WebSocket always fails on
relay-mode instances, leaving users permanently offline.

With ['polling','websocket']: session establishes via polling, then
Socket.IO probes for WebSocket upgrade. On relay instances the probe
fails gracefully (stays on polling). On direct instances it upgrades
transparently. Both cases work correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
test: fix weak assertions — replace expect(true) and toBeDefined-only tests (WOP-1925) (#82)

* test: fix weak assertions in engine and schema tests (WOP-1925)

- Replace expect(true).toBe(true) with expect(reapExpired).toHaveBeenCalled()
- Replace toBeDefined()-only checks in schema.test.ts with getTableName() assertions
- Replace .some().toBe(true) patterns with toContainEqual/toContain across all test files
- Fix conditional guard pattern in engine.test.ts transitioned event assertion
- Strengthen invocationId, spawned, clearCall, and server property assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: fix remaining weak assertions flagged in review (WOP-1925)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: convert remaining .some().toBe(true) to strong assertion (WOP-1925)

Replace weak .some(m => m.includes(...)).toBe(true) with
expect.arrayContaining + stringContaining matching exact error
messages for fromState and toState validation on empty-flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore strong assertions for all weak toContain regressions (WOP-1925)

Replace all join("; ").toContain() weak patterns with .some().toBe(true)
so each assertion verifies a single message contains the required substring,
not that the substring appears anywhere across joined messages.
